body:
## 综合 Review — PR #102

综合多个 review 意见, 按严重度分级。

| 级别 | 数量 | 关键项 |
|------|------|--------|
| **Must Fix** | 5 | 同步阻塞, 死代码, 半功能实例, update回滚, summarize阻塞 |
| **Should Fix** | 5 | 封装破坏, JSON脆弱, upper() bug, MEMORY.md无上限, required冲突 |
| **Suggestion** | 3 | DSN正则, scanFiles缓存, HTTP连接池 |

comments.body: **Must Fix — Sync HTTP blocks event loop**: `httpx.Client(timeout=60.0)` is a synchronous client. When `_build_default_llm_fns()` is called from `create_memory_manager()` inside a FastAPI async request handler, this blocks the entire asyncio event loop for up to 60 seconds. Fix: wrap in `asyncio.to_thread()` or switch to `httpx.AsyncClient`.

**Must Fix — Sync DB query**: `create_engine()` + `Session().execute()` performs a synchronous database query that blocks the async event loop. Move this to `main.py` lifespan so the default LLM config is preloaded at startup.

**Must Fix — Dead code**: `_classify_type()` (lines 642-666) and `_classify_memory_destination()` (lines 668-716) are defined but never called anywhere. `_save_decision()` already handles type/name/description/target in one shot. Remove or mark as reserved.

**Must Fix — update() missing rollback**: `_create_new_entry()` (line 286) rolls back file write when index update fails (`store.delete_file()`). But `update()` (line 323) only logs a warning when index update fails after writing the file - the file content has changed but the index is out of sync.

**Must Fix — _summarize_content() blocks**: `self._llm_query(prompt)` is a synchronous HTTP call inside `save()` / `update()` paths. Each memory_save triggers a blocking network request. Fix: `asyncio.to_thread(self._llm_query, prompt)`.

**Should Fix — Fragile JSON parsing**: `text.strip()` + `json.loads()` fails when LLM adds text outside the JSON block (e.g. `Here is the result: { ... }`). Use `re.search(r'\{[\s\S]*\}', text)` to extract the JSON object.

**Should Fix — upper() breaks filename case**: `decision["target_filename"] = ...strip().upper()` converts LLM-returned filenames (e.g. `user-preference.md`) to uppercase (`USER-PREFERENCE.MD`), then passes them directly to `self.store.read_file(target)`. On case-sensitive filesystems (Linux production), this fails. Use `.upper()` only for the NEW comparison, keep original case for read_file().
**Suggestion — scan_files() per call**: `all_entries = self.store.scan_files()` scans the filesystem on every `save()`. Add mtime cache (30s TTL) to reduce I/O overhead as memory count grows.

**Should Fix — DSN URL conversion**: `sync_url.replace(prefix, "")` can corrupt URLs when the prefix substring appears in passwords or hostnames. Use regex: `re.sub(r'^\w+\+(?:aiosqlite|asyncpg)://', ..., url)`

**Should Fix — Breaks encapsulation**: `existing._llm_query = ...`, `existing.ranker._llm_query = ...`, `existing.extractor._run_agent = ...` directly mutate private attributes. Add a public `update_llm_fns()` method to MemoryManage instead.

**Must Fix — Half-functional instance**: `_ensure_memory_manager()` calls `create_memory_manager(memory_root)` without `llm_query_fn`, leaving the memory manager with no LLM capability (no summarization, ranking, or dedup) in the chat path. Initialize with `llm_query_fn` in `main.py` lifespan.

**Should Fix — MEMORY.md no line limit**: `f.read()` loads the entire MEMORY.md into the system prompt on every request. With 100+ entries this can exceed context window. Add `splitlines()[:50]` line limit.

**Should Fix — required conflicts with filename param**: `required = ["query"]` but the new `filename` parameter can read a memory directly without a query. Either make required empty or add custom validation (at least one of query or filename required).
